AMENDED PROOF OF SERVICE (TRANSACTION ID # 56049743) FILED BY PLAINTIFF THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE CALIFORNIA BY AND THROUGH DENNIS J. HERRERA, CITY ATTORNEY FOR THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS TAMARA YUROVSKY, LEV YUROVSKY, TATYANA YUROVSKY, LEV AND TATYANA YUROVSKY, AS TRUSTEES' MOTION TO QUASH PLAINTIFF'S SUBPOENAS DUCES TECUM TO AIRBNB, INC. IS DENIED. AIRBNB IS NOT A "WITNESS" AS DEFINED UNDER CAL. CODE OF CIV. PRO. SECTION 1985.3. THEREFORE, PLAINTIFF WAS NOT REQUIRED TO PROVIDE A CONSUMER NOTICE PRIOR TO SERVING THE SUBPOENAS. FURTHERMORE, THE INFORMATION REQUESTED DOES NOT IMPERMISSIBLY INFRINGE ON DEFENDANTS' PRIVACY, FINANCIAL OR OTHERWISE. THE COURT ALSO FINDS THAT THE SUBPOENAS DO NOT INFRINGE UPON DOCUMENTS PROTECTED BY THE ATTORNEY WORK-PRODUCT DOCTRINE. NOR DOES THE COURT FIND THAT THE SUBPOENAS ARE OVERBROAD IN TIME, AS THE DURATION OF THE ALLEGED WRONGFUL CONDUCT IS RELEVANT TO THE DETERMINATION OF A PROPER CIVIL PENALTY. (SEE CAL. BUS. & PROF. CODE 17206(B).) MOREOVER, DEFENDANTS' ARGUMENTS ON FIRST AMENDMENT GROUNDS ARE MOOT IN LIGHT OF THE AGREEMENT BETWEEN PLAINTIFF AND AIRBNB THAT THIRD PARTIES WILL NOT BE IDENTIFIED BY NAME IN AIRBNB'S INITIAL PRODUCTION IN RESPONSE TO THE SUBPOENAS. ADDITIONALLY, PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO THE DOCUMENTS SUBMITTED WITH THE SECOND SUPPLEMENTAL DECLARATION ARE MOOT BECAUSE IDENTITIES OF USERS WILL NOT BE REVEALED IN AIRBNB'S INITIAL PRODUCTION. DEFENDANTS' REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. FINALLY, PLAINTIFF'S REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED WITH MODIFICATION. PLAINTIFF IS AWARDED $7,250, BASED ON 35 HOURS OF ATTORNEY TIME, PAYABLE TO THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: BRENDA L. CROW, CSR# 10503, T:415.834.1114 (302/EHG)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-17-2014 Continued To Feb-04-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motin To Quash Pltff'S Subpoenas Duces Tecum To Airbnb, Inc
Off Calendar Oct-03-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Aleksandr A. Volkov, Esq. of Volkov Law Firm, T:415.987.7000, for defendants and moving party;
Megan Cesare-Eastman, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, T:415.554.3970, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative, with modification as to the amount of sanctions. DEFENDANTs TAMARA YUROVSKY, LEV YUROVSKY, TATYANA YUROVSKY, LEV AND TATYANA YUROVSKY, AS TRUSTEES' Motion To Quash Plaintiff's Subpoenas Duces Tecum To Airbnb, Inc. is denied. AirBnB is not a "witness" as defined under Cal. Code of Civ. Pro. Section 1985.3. Therefore, Plaintiff was not required to provide a consumer notice prior to serving the subpoenas. Furthermore, the information requested does not impermissibly infringe on Defendants' privacy, financial or otherwise. The Court also finds that the subpoenas do not infringe upon documents protected by the attorney work-product doctrine. Nor does the Court find that the subpoenas are overbroad in time, as the duration of the alleged wrongful conduct is relevant to the determination of a proper civil penalty. (See Cal. Bus. & Prof. Code 17206(b).) Moreover, Defendants' arguments on First Amendment grounds are moot in light of the agreement between Plaintiff and AirBnB that third parties will not be identified by name in AirBnB's initial production in response to the subpoenas. Additionally, Plaintiff's objections to the documents submitted with the second supplemental declaration are moot because identities of users will not be revealed in AirBnB's initial production. Defendants' request for judicial notice is granted. Finally, Plaintiff's requests for sanctions is granted with modification. Plaintiff is awarded $7,250, based on 35 hours of attorney time, payable to the City and County of San Francisco. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Brenda L. Crow, CSR# 10503, T:415.834.1114
